div1.entryFree: Trench, vb. to cut: the wide wound that the boar had --ed in his soft flank, Ven. 1052. a figure --ed in ice, Gent. III, 2, 7. with twenty --ed gashes on his head, Mcb. III, 4, 27. cf. Entrench. == to dig, to make furrows: no more shall --ing war channel her fields, H4A I, 1, 7.* == to drain by digging, to turn off and divert from the usual channel: a little charge will t. him here (the river Trent) H4A III, 1, 112.
